summary: A solid audience tape with occasional hum and channel quirks captures a legendary Bronx show highlighted by blistering Bertha, St. Stephen and Hard To Handle.
review: The recording is an audience‑sourced tape that suffers from some static, cable hum and left/right balance issues, yet it remains listenable and conveys the raw energy of the outdoor Bronx concert.  Musically the night is remembered for a thunderous Bertha, a soaring St. Stephen, a punchy Good Lovin', and one of the best‑heard Hard To Handle performances of ’71, with Pigpen’s vocals and Jerry’s lead work standing out despite mix quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈4.3; most comments praise the performance while noting only moderate recording flaws, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Occasional cable hum, pop/dropouts and left‑right channel imbalance (especially on early discs). Jerry’s guitar sits low in the mix, Phil’s bass sometimes too loud, but overall the tape captures the live atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set outdoor summer show with a high‑energy opening, strong second set jams and a memorable encore; the crowd vibe and spontaneous stage banter are frequently highlighted.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar is often buried in the mix but his vocals and lead work are described as soaring and full of energy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass levels swing – sometimes too loud, other times nicely audible, with listeners noting solid bass lines when balanced.
